title: Se pregătește oare a treia suspendare?
summary: The article discusses the possibility of a third presidential suspension in Romania, triggered by a legal challenge from AUR lawyer Uscov against Constitutional Court judge Dacian Dragoș's qualifications. Author Cristian Preda argues that Dragoș's legal teaching experience at a political science faculty fully qualifies as 'higher legal education,' and warns that AUR leader Simion is using this legal maneuver as a stepping stone to suspend President Nicușor Dan.
talking_points: AUR lawyer Uscov challenged CCR judge Dacian Dragoș's appointment, claiming his teaching experience in a political science faculty doesn't count as legal education

Preda argues that constitutional law and other legal disciplines are integral to political science faculties, citing jurist Iulia Motoc who taught exclusively at such a faculty before serving at CCR, ECHR, and ICC
AUR has already collected signatures to initiate a third presidential suspension and would need half of PSD's parliamentary votes to succeed
The political calculation relies on a Court of Appeals ruling against Dragoș, which would delegitimize President Nicușor Dan's appointment process
PSD could use a favorable court ruling as political cover to join the suspension effort while claiming to defend rule of law
author: Cristian Preda
